title: auth: challenge nonce has no expiry; relay accepts stale nonces
description: new_challenge() generates a random 32-byte nonce but the relay never enforces a time bound on when it must be used. The relay sends the challenge and then waits indefinitely (no timeout filed separately). If an attacker delays sending authenticate by hours, the nonce is still accepted. The nonce serves only as replay-protection for that session, not a time-bounded token. Unlike PoW tokens (which have ts_floor expiry), auth nonces are valid for the lifetime of the TCP connection. If connection teardown is slow or an intermediary buffers the frame, the nonce window is unbounded. At minimum, document this as an accepted limitation; at best, add a per-nonce timestamp and reject nonces older than 30s even if the connection is live.
